_note: Heading-capability OVERLAY (FL-30, APF22 Section D taxonomy). NON-DESTRUCTIVE: this registry groups the flat leaf capabilities under heading capabilities as METADATA only — no dataset is migrated, merged, or renamed ('the capabilities must not be crushed'). Each member_capabilities entry must resolve to a real capability (enforced by test/contract-pharm-capability-groups.js). NTI-as-bucket is expressed here: the 'therapeutic_drug_monitoring' group lists nti (the narrow-index bucket) alongside tdm_parameters; the frozen pharm-check nti_check is unchanged. The heading taxonomy is cited to APF22 (structure only). The overlay GROWS as Priority-2/3 capabilities (hepatic, elderly, pregnancy, breastfeeding, dispensing_considerations, discolouration) are built.

groups.title: Indications
groups.description: What the medicine is used for.

groups.title: Counselling
groups.description: APF Counselling heading — advisory labels, consumer counselling messages, and low-tier cautions. precautions is incorporated here as its own leaf (not merged).

groups.title: Dispensing considerations
groups.description: APF Dispensing considerations heading — formulation handling ('should not be crushed') and available forms.

groups.title: Safety & contraindications
groups.description: Interactions, serious toxicity, absolute/strong contraindications, and allergy cross-reactivity.

groups.title: Special populations
groups.description: Population-specific handling. Grows with further Priority-2/3 capabilities (elderly, breastfeeding).

groups.title: Therapeutic drug monitoring
groups.description: APF TDM heading. nti is the narrow-therapeutic-index BUCKET; tdm_parameters carries the monitoring ranges/timing. The frozen pharm-check nti_check reads the nti bucket unchanged.

groups.title: Dosing
groups.description: Dose guidance (the AU dose — the ONLY member the engine may read), the cited dose-evidence reference register, the §4.3b review queue for APF dose misses, and international_dose_guidance (US/EU approved LABEL doses from AMASS RegulatoryCore). NOTE the group's source_ref describes the HEADING's origin (APF22 Section D), not its members' sources — international_dose_guidance is sourced from amass-regulatory and is ENGINE-ISOLATED: a foreign label can never become an AU dose. It exists so a clinician can read the US/EU label beside the AU dose, and so dose_guidance.au_congruence can appraise against it. The appraisal annotates; it never vetoes.

groups.title: Regulatory / reference
groups.description: AU scheduling and the PBS formulary.
